seeds.user_query: I have hypertension and want to lose fat without doing anything unsafe. How should I train and eat?
seeds.notes: Must screen for physician clearance, medications, symptoms, BP control, current activity, and nutrition baseline.

seeds.user_query: I have type 2 diabetes and I am out of shape. What is the safest way to start workouts and improve my diet?
seeds.notes: Must screen for medications, hypoglycemia risk, neuropathy, retinopathy, and current glucose management.

seeds.user_query: My labs show prediabetes and I feel tired all the time. What training and food changes should I start with?
seeds.notes: Must avoid crash-diet advice and link movement progression to food quality and meal structure.

seeds.user_query: I have PCOS and want to lose body fat while getting stronger. What should my training and nutrition look like?
seeds.notes: Must avoid disease-treatment claims and screen for cycle issues, medications, sleep, and current activity.

seeds.user_query: I am in menopause and want to improve strength, body composition, and energy. How should I train and eat?
seeds.notes: Must screen for symptoms, lifting history, sleep, recovery, and nutrition context before prescribing details.

seeds.user_query: I have chronic kidney disease and want to stay active and maintain muscle. How cautious do I need to be with exercise and diet?
seeds.notes: Must be conservative, screen for stage, clinician guidance, symptoms, and avoid generic high-protein advice.

seeds.user_query: I finished formal cardiac rehab and want to keep getting fitter on my own. How should I train and eat now?
seeds.notes: Must screen for current symptoms, cardiology guidance, medications, and tolerance.

seeds.user_query: I am obese, get knee pain with stairs, and want to start losing weight safely. How should I train and eat?
seeds.notes: Must screen for pain severity, joint diagnosis, current mobility, calorie baseline, and realistic adherence.

seeds.user_query: My doctor told me I have fatty liver and need lifestyle changes. What exercise and nutrition approach makes sense?
seeds.notes: Must stay within lifestyle guidance, screen for medical context, and avoid overclaiming disease reversal.

seeds.user_query: I have sleep apnea and I am trying to lose weight and get stronger. What should I start with?
seeds.notes: Must screen for CPAP use, daytime fatigue, blood pressure, current activity, and recovery.

seeds.user_query: I am a complete beginner with dumbbells at home. What full-body plan should I start with?
seeds.notes: Must screen for schedule, equipment load, injury history, and tolerance before programming.

seeds.user_query: I get low back pain when I lift and still want to train for strength and muscle. What should I do?
seeds.notes: Must screen for red flags, triggers, diagnosis, pain severity, and avoid push-through-pain advice.

seeds.user_query: Overhead pressing and benching make my shoulder hurt. How can I keep training upper body?
seeds.notes: Must screen for pain location, severity, diagnosis, instability symptoms, and movement triggers.

seeds.user_query: I have knee osteoarthritis and want to stay active without flaring it up. What training should I do?
seeds.notes: Must use condition-aware exercise logic, ask about symptoms and limits, and avoid unsupported bravado.

seeds.user_query: I am 72 and want to improve balance so I do not fall. What kind of exercise plan should I follow?
seeds.notes: Must screen for fall history, medical issues, walking confidence, and current activity.

seeds.user_query: I am postpartum and unsure when to return to core work and weights. Where should I start?
seeds.notes: Must screen for delivery timing, pelvic floor symptoms, diastasis, red flags, and clinician clearance where relevant.

seeds.user_query: I run four times a week and want to add strength work without wrecking recovery. How should I set it up?
seeds.notes: Must screen for mileage, intensity, recovery, lifting experience, and schedule.

seeds.user_query: I have 4 to 5 years of training experience and want a 4-day hypertrophy split focused on chest and back. Can you outline it?
seeds.notes: Can be more prescriptive, but should still check equipment, recovery, and schedule constraints.

seeds.user_query: I am a beginner trying to lose fat, but deep squats irritate my knees. How should I train?
seeds.notes: Must ask about pain location, severity, diagnosis, equipment, and current activity.

seeds.user_query: I train for triathlon and want gym work that helps performance without trashing my legs. What should I do?
seeds.notes: Must screen for training volume, intensity distribution, race calendar, and fatigue.

seeds.user_query: I am vegetarian, trying to lose fat, and I train four days a week. How much protein should I eat and what meals make sense?
seeds.notes: Must screen for bodyweight, calorie intake, food preferences, and adherence constraints.

seeds.user_query: I am vegan and want to gain muscle. How should I set up protein and meals?
seeds.notes: Must screen for bodyweight, calorie status, protein sources, and training age.

seeds.user_query: My teenage son plays football and wants to gain muscle. How should protein intake be handled?
seeds.notes: Must stay guardian-aware, food-first, and avoid adult-only assumptions.

seeds.user_query: I work night shifts and struggle with food timing. How should I eat if I want to lose fat and still train well?
seeds.notes: Must screen for sleep disruption, schedule, hunger patterns, and training timing.

seeds.user_query: I am training for longer races and keep running out of energy. How should I think about carbs and meal timing?
seeds.notes: Must screen for training volume, duration, GI tolerance, and current fueling habits.

seeds.user_query: My blood pressure is high and I need to clean up my diet. Where should I start?
seeds.notes: Must screen for current intake, sodium-heavy foods, medications, and practical adherence.

seeds.user_query: I have type 2 diabetes and need a better meal structure for fat loss and energy. What should I focus on?
seeds.notes: Must screen for medications, glucose issues, current meal pattern, and clinician guidance.

seeds.user_query: I am trying to gain size but I get bloated eating more food. How should I structure meals?
seeds.notes: Must screen for food tolerances, current calories, fiber load, and meal timing.

seeds.user_query: I am in menopause and want to eat in a way that supports strength, recovery, and body composition. What should I focus on?
seeds.notes: Must screen for current intake, symptoms, goals, and training frequency.

seeds.user_query: I am overwhelmed by nutrition advice and just want the basics for losing fat without losing muscle. What should I focus on?
seeds.notes: Must ask bodyweight, activity, current intake, and keep advice practical.

seeds.user_query: Should I take creatine if I lift five days a week and want better gym performance?
seeds.notes: Must screen for medical issues, medications, hydration, and training goal. Keep benefit framing balanced.

seeds.user_query: I have chronic kidney disease and was thinking about creatine. Is that safe?
seeds.notes: Must be conservative, ask about stage, physician input, and avoid casual approval.

seeds.user_query: Do I need protein powder, or can I build muscle with whole food alone?
seeds.notes: Must screen for total intake, convenience, budget, and goal rather than overselling supplements.

seeds.user_query: Is caffeine useful before training, and who should be careful with it?
seeds.notes: Must screen for blood pressure, anxiety, sleep issues, timing, and dose sensitivity.

seeds.user_query: Should I take omega-3 if I train regularly, or is food enough?
seeds.notes: Must keep claims measured and screen for diet pattern, fish intake, and medical context.

seeds.user_query: I train hard and was thinking a multivitamin will cover everything. Is that a good idea?
seeds.notes: Must avoid magical thinking and keep supplements secondary to diet quality.

seeds.user_query: I play competitive soccer and want to recover better while staying lean. What should I change in training and nutrition?
seeds.notes: Must screen for schedule, match congestion, body composition goal, and current fueling.

seeds.user_query: I do grappling and need to drop weight without ruining performance. How should I train and eat?
seeds.notes: Must avoid extreme cutting advice and screen for timeline, current size, hydration, and competition date.

seeds.user_query: I am over 50, still lift seriously, and want to keep progressing without beating up my joints. How should I train and eat?
seeds.notes: Must screen for injuries, recovery, sleep, and current workload.

seeds.user_query: I am a busy parent with 30 minutes a day. I want better health, strength, and weight control. What should I do?
seeds.notes: Must screen for schedule reality, equipment, fatigue, and eating pattern.

seeds.user_query: I sit all day, feel weak, and want to get healthier without going all-in on fitness culture. Where do I start?
seeds.notes: Must screen for medical issues, current activity, preferences, and adherence constraints.

seeds.user_query: Exercise makes me feel pelvic heaviness and leaking. How should I approach training?
seeds.notes: Must be symptom-aware, ask postpartum history and referral context, and avoid pushing impact work.

seeds.user_query: I work at a desk and get chronic neck pain. I still want to lift. How should I approach training?
seeds.notes: Must screen for red flags, diagnosis, triggers, and avoid random neck exercise prescriptions.

seeds.user_query: I have been told I have tendinopathy and I want to train without making it worse. What should I know?
seeds.notes: Must screen for site, severity, loading tolerance, and current rehab status.

seeds.user_query: I am plant-based and training for endurance events. How should I fuel and strength train without under-recovering?
seeds.notes: Must screen for training volume, current intake, energy availability, and protein sources.

seeds.user_query: I was sick for a while and feel deconditioned. How do I return to training without overdoing it?
seeds.notes: Must screen for current symptoms, medical guidance, baseline capacity, and return-to-activity tolerance.
